spots.name: King-high dry: range advantage
spots.description: Hero (OOP) holds top set and bets 1/2 to pot for value into a capped bluff-catching range. The lesson: how a clean range advantage on a dry board lets you bet often and large.

spots.name: Ace-high dry: top-pair kicker battle
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets top pair good kicker (AQ) on a dry ace-high board, while IP hides sets. Kicker quality decides who bets thin and who bluff-catches.

spots.name: Queen-high dry: two-pair value
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets queen-jack two pair for value on a disconnected high-card board; bluff-catching queens have to decide how light to call.

spots.name: King-queen broadway dry
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets king-queen two pair on a dry two-broadway board; missed straight draws make the natural bluffs.

spots.name: Jack-high dry: thin value
spots.description: Hero (OOP) makes a thin half-pot value bet with top pair (AJ) on a low jack-high board where worse pairs still call.

spots.name: Ten-high dry: overpair value
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets an overpair / top set on a ten-high board where every value bet is thin and overcards make the bluffs.

spots.name: Ace-high dry: weak-ace defense
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets a weak ace small for thin value and to deny equity; the lesson is sizing down with a marginal made hand.

spots.name: Paired aces: kicker war
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets trip aces with the best kicker; weaker trips bluff-catch and busted broadways bluff. Paired-ace boards are a pure kicker contest.

spots.name: Paired sixes: overpairs rule
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets an overpair / trips on a paired low board topped by a king; middle pairs have to call or fold to pressure.

spots.name: Paired kings: trips vs overpair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets trip kings, picking a small or polar size; the lesson is whether IP's overpairs are value or just expensive bluff-catchers on a paired-king board.

spots.name: Board pairs eights: boats freeze pairs
spots.description: The river pairs the eight. Hero (OOP) value bets aces-up and the boat (88) while medium pairs are frozen into bluff-catchers.

spots.name: Trips on board: kicker plays
spots.description: With trips on the board the river is a kicker battle. Hero (OOP) value bets the ace / overpair; everything else bluff-catches or gives up.

spots.name: 3-bet pot, paired queens: overpair vs trips
spots.description: In a 3-bet pot, Hero (OOP) bets an overpair into paired queens, balancing against the caller's slow-played trips. The bloated pot rewards getting value in.

spots.name: Two-tone broadway brick: two-pair value
spots.description: The diamond draw bricks. Hero (OOP) bets ace-queen two pair 3/4 pot for value while missed flush draws look for fold equity.

spots.name: Two-tone king board: top pair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets top pair / trip tens after the flush draw misses; IP defends second pair and weaker kings.

spots.name: Two-tone queen-high: set and top pair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets a set / top pair on a queen-high two-tone board where no flush is possible; thin value against weaker queens.

spots.name: Two-tone ace-ten: two pair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets ace-ten two pair on a two-tone ace-high board; weaker aces and sets decide how much to pay.

spots.name: Two-tone jack-ten: two pair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets jack-ten two pair on a connected but flush-impossible board; one-pair hands have to find the right calls.

spots.name: Flush possible: overpair vs made flush
spots.description: Three hearts are out. Hero (OOP) value bets a made flush while bare aces / overpairs are demoted to bluff-catchers.

spots.name: Flush board: nut advantage
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets the nut flush; who holds the bigger flushes dictates who bets big and who must check-call.

spots.name: King-high flush board
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets the ace-high flush; top set without a heart drops to a bluff-catcher against the smaller flushes.

spots.name: Low flush board: nut flush vs set
spots.description: Three low clubs. Hero (OOP) bets the nut / strong flush for stacks while a rivered set of aces tries to bluff-catch.

spots.name: Broadway flush board
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets the nut flush (or king-queen two pair) on a high two-tone-to-flush board; smaller diamonds bluff-catch.

spots.name: KQJ broadway: straights and two pair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets the broadway straight (AT) and two pair; on this connected high board, sets and overpairs become bluff-catchers against the straight.

spots.name: Ace-king broadway: top two pair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets ace-king top two pair on a dry ace-king board; the caller is capped at one pair and must defend.

spots.name: Queen-jack broadway: two pair
spots.description: The river jack adds a second broadway. Hero (OOP) value bets queen-jack two pair while one-pair hands bluff-catch.

spots.name: Ace-queen broadway: top two pair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets ace-queen top two pair; weaker aces and queens are bluff-catchers on this dry broadway runout.

spots.name: Low board: set vs straight threat
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets a set on a low board, but must respect IP's rivered eight-high straight; thin value meets the nuts.

spots.name: Low jack-high: overpairs out of position
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets overpairs and top pair on a low, disconnected jack-high board where thin value goes a long way.

spots.name: Low paired board: trips vs overpair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets an overpair on a low paired board, but IP's trip sevens and fours full lurk in the calling range -- a lesson in pot control vs thin value.

spots.name: 3-bet pot, dry ace-high
spots.description: In a 3-bet pot, Hero (OOP) value bets top pair top kicker (AK) on a dry ace-high board; the caller's queens bluff-catch for a swollen pot.

spots.name: 3-bet pot, low board: overpair vs set
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets an overpair in a 3-bet pot on a low board topped by a king, navigating the caller's flopped sets.

spots.name: 3-bet pot, paired ace river: trips
spots.description: The river pairs the ace in a 3-bet pot. Hero (OOP) value bets trip aces with a strong kicker while overpairs bluff-catch.

spots.name: 3-bet pot, broadway: top two pair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) value bets ace-king top two pair in a 3-bet pot on a broadway board; the caller's weaker two pair and sets defend.

spots.name: 3-bet pot, two-tone: top two and overpair
spots.description: Hero (OOP) bets ace-jack top two pair / queens overpair in a 3-bet pot after the flush draw bricks; IP defends jacks-up and middling pairs.
